body.title: Shim bus watchdog: reconnect on sustained Connection refused
body.result: WorkerListener counts failures, shim reconnects bus in-place after 5; gives up + removes flag after 3 failed reconnects; 8 tests
body.intention: I intend that when any device shim detects sustained bus connection failures, it automatically attempts reconnection so devices recover without manual intervention.
body.description:
DickSimnel (and any shim using the bus) currently loops indefinitely on 'Connection refused' with no recovery attempt. The shim should detect N consecutive fetch failures, attempt to rebuild the bus connection via `make_bus_connection()`, and if reconnect also fails after M attempts, set device status to 'fault' and remove the availability flag.

Pattern: add `_consecutive_failures` counter to the WorkerListener poll loop. After 5 consecutive failures, call back to the shim's `restart()` which re-runs `_connect_bus()`. If reconnect succeeds, reset counter and resume. After 3 failed restart attempts, call `stop()` and write a fault log entry — don't keep spinning.

Apply the same pattern to DickSimnelShim first; document it in BaseShim as the recommended watchdog pattern for any bus-using shim.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/worker_listener.py, devices/dicksimnel/shim.py, unseen_university/shim.py (doc only)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN — reconnect logic in DickSimnel worker listener + shim. OUT — fixing Dovecot not running (infrastructure, not code); applying to other shims (follow-on).
**Completion criteria:** Unit test: listener with a bus that raises ConnectionRefusedError 5 times triggers shim.restart(); listener with 3*5 failures calls shim.stop(); normal operation resumes after successful reconnect.
